content: Bioavailability is measured by comparing AUC after non-IV administration to AUC after IV administration of the same dose: F = (AUC_oral × Dose_IV) / (AUC_IV × Dose_oral). Bioequivalence studies compare test vs reference formulations; two products are bioequivalent if 90% CI of geometric mean ratio for AUC and Cmax falls within 80-125%. Absolute bioavailability requires IV comparator; relative bioavailability compares two non-IV formulations. Factors affecting measurement: sampling duration must cover elimination phase; assay sensitivity must detect low concentrations; subjects should be fasting unless food-effect study. Generic approval requires demonstrating bioequivalence to brand-name product.
